chapter_title.ar: باب أَنَّ الْمُطَلَّقَةَ وَ هُوَ غَائِبٌ عَنْهَا تَعْتَدُّ مِنْ يَوْمَ طُلِّقَتْ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 41 – The divorced woman, and he is absent from her, would observe the waiting period from the day she is divorced

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/190)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (21/190)
text: قَالَ فِي الْمُطَلَّقَةِ إِذَا قَامَتِ الْبَيِّنَةُ أَنَّهُ قَدْ طَلَّقَهَا مُنْذُ كَذَا وَ كَذَا فَكَانَتْ عِدَّتُهَا قَدِ انْقَضَتْ فَقَدْ بَانَتْ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Nasr,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Al-Hassan Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> having said regarding the divorced woman: ‘When the proof is established for her that he has divorced her since such and such (a day), so her waiting period had passed, so she is irrevocably divorced’.
en.sarwar: 6. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu Nasr who has narrated the following: “Abu al-Hassan al-Rida’, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If proof is established that she was divorced on such and such date which proves the expiration of her ‘Iddah (waiting period) she becomes a stranger for him.’”
